Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

must_detect.matched_text:
Confidential Term Sheet — Strategic Convertible Murabaha into Al‑Nour Logistics PJSC (CR 4031234567) by Sahara Capital SPC (CR 7012345678), Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
Securities and size: SAR 180,000,000 murabaha facility converting into ordinary shares at SAR 14.50/share, subject to CMA approval and T a d a w u l regulatory review.
Use of proceeds: fleet expansion and warehouse automation in Riyadh and Jeddah; no funds used for dividends for 12 months; Shariah board of Falcon Bank KSA to opine.
Pricing and adjustments: conversion price resets if EBITDA miss >10% vs draft Q2‑Q4 2026 forecast (non‑public, issuer MNPI) and triggers a 5% warrant sweetener.
Conditions precedent: C M A  F i l e  R e f MC/26/00971 clearance; auditors’ comfort letter; SDAIA PDPL cross‑border transfer approval for diligence data hosted in eu‑central‑1.
Governance: Investor board observer and, post‑conversion, one seat to be nominated by Sahara Capital (Ms. Rania Al‑Safwa, Partner, rania.al‑safwa@saharacap.sa, +966‑11‑555‑8123).
Management disclosure (restricted): Mr. Faisal Al‑Miraj (CFO, Al‑Nour), born 1987‑05‑14 (age 38), based Jeddah‑North Obhur, work email fa.miraj@alnourlogistics.sa, device NajmTab‑4G (Wi‑Fi B8:2D:••:9A), Iqama ending ••0356, VPN sess‑9afc2d19, office ext. 2241.
Data room access: https://dealroom.falconbank.sa/sso?acct=ACCT_JD‑4821&user=f.miraj&sess=9afc2d19‑k‑alpha&cr=4031234567 (credentials remain issuer property; access logged).
Representations: Issuer confirms CR, tax registration, and no undisclosed related‑party transactions; any material contracts uploaded remain MNPI until C M A posting, subject to CMA Market Conduct follow‑up on disclosure controls.
Data protection: PDPL compliance covenant; personal data minimized and pseudonymized; any transfer outside KSA to Sahara Capital’s Bahrain office requires SDAIA transfer mechanism and controller approval.
Negative clarification: this document does not collect MAC addresses; product names like NajmTab‑4G and GhadanPhone are mentioned for asset inventory only, not to identify anyone.
Generic staffing note (non‑identifying): a mid‑level engineer, late‑20s to early‑30s, based in Eastern Province, using a GhadanPhone 12 and laptop model Rimal‑E3, may support integration; no names or IDs.
Process: next steps—update gantt, ping legal, post in teams; references to public Tadawul listing handbook and generic KSA corporate governance code are not personal data.
Closing and long‑stop: expected signing 30 Sha’ban 1447H; long‑stop 90 days; governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; disputes to be resolved by arbitration in Riyadh under SCCA rules.
